title: Adenandra marginata
taxonomy: Adenandra marginata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, which is commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This classification suggests that Adenandra marginata shares some characteristics and genetic traits with other plants in the Rutaceae family. More specifically, Adenandra marginata is a member of the genus Adenandra, which is a group of plants native to southern Africa. Within the genus, Adenandra marginata is considered a distinct species due to its unique characteristics and features. Further classification may include the plant's order, class, and kingdom, but its family and genus are well-established.
